title: 7 Herbal Essential Oils For Difficulty Eating
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils can be very helpful if you're having trouble eating. Sometimes, people struggle to eat due to digestive issues, stress, or a lack of appetite. Herbal teas made from certain herbs can help alleviate these problems. For example, Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe digestive issues and make eating more comfortable. Drinking ginger tea can also help stimulate digestion and improve appetite.  Fennel (Foeniculum vulgare) is another herb that can be beneficial when eaten or drunk as a tea. It's known for its ability to ease digestive discomfort and nausea, making it a great option for people who struggle to eat due to stomach problems. Fennel tea can also help to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines, making digestion easier.  Lavender (Lavandula angustifolia) is often thought of as a calming herb, and it can be very helpful for people who struggle to eat due to anxiety or stress. Drinking lavender tea can help to calm the mind and body, making it easier to feel hungry and enjoy eating. Lavender also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and make eating more comfortable.  Using herbal teas like these can bring several benefits to your life. Not only can they help to alleviate digestive issues and make eating more comfortable, but they can also help to promote a healthy and balanced diet. By choosing to drink herbal teas instead of relying on medication or other treatments, you can also avoid potential side effects and take a more natural approach to managing your digestive health.
intro_image_alt: herbal essential oils for difficulty eating

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils contains active constituents like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce digestive discomfort. The oils' warming and relaxing effects can ease nausea and stomach cramps, making it easier to eat and digest food. The analgesic and anti-spasmodic properties of gingerol help calm stomach muscles and reduce spasms, allowing food to move through the digestive system smoothly. This can be especially helpful for people experiencing dyspepsia, nausea, or other digestive issues that make eating difficult. By reducing digestive discomfort and inflammation, Zingiber officinale essential oils can help improve appetite and make eating more comfortable.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for difficulty eating
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Ginger roots and 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Peel the ginger roots and chop them into small pieces.
Boil the water in the saucepan with the ginger pieces for 1 hour.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl. Discard the ginger pieces.
Use a distillation apparatus or a still to separate the essential oil from the water. Collect the oil in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare essential oils contains compounds like anethole and fenchone, which give it digestive and carminative properties. These properties help relieve discomfort and gas that can make eating difficult. Foeniculum vulgare essential oils also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can soothe the digestive tract and reduce swelling that can cause eating problems. The anti-spasmodic properties of fenchone can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, allowing for more comfortable eating. When inhaled, the anethole in Foeniculum vulgare essential oils can also help calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety that can contribute to difficulty eating.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal essential oils for difficulty eating
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Foeniculum vulgare seeds and 1 cup of carrier oil (like jojoba or sweet almond oil).
Heat 2 cups of water in a saucepan over low heat. Add 1/4 cup of Foeniculum vulgare seeds and let it steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a bowl and discard the seeds. Add 1 cup of carrier oil to the bowl.
Let the mixture sit for 2-3 days, shaking the bowl occasionally, until the oil separates from the water.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container. Store the Foeniculum vulgare essential oil in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils contains linalool and linalyl acetate as its main bioactive constituents. These compounds have a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress that can contribute to difficulty eating. The sedative properties of linalool and linalyl acetate help to relax the body and mind, making it easier to focus on eating and digesting food. Lavandula angustifolia essential oils also contains beta-caryophyllene, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and promote a healthy gut. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, Lavandula angustifolia essential oils can help to alleviate difficulty eating.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for difficulty eating
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Lavandula angustifolia flowers, a clean glass jar, and a carrier oil like jojoba oil.
Combine 2 cups of water and 1 cup of sugar in a saucepan. Heat the mixture over medium heat, stirring until the sugar dissolves.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool. Then, pour the mixture over the Lavandula angustifolia flowers in the glass jar.
Let the mixture sit for 2-3 days, allowing the flowers to infuse into the liquid. Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar.
Combine the infused liquid with jojoba oil (1:1 ratio) and store the mixture in a cool, dark place. Let it sit for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily, before straining and using the essential oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils contains high levels of menthone, limonene, and menthol, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. These properties help to ease digestion and reduce nausea, making it easier to eat. The menthone in Mentha x piperita essential oils has a calming effect on the stomach muscles, allowing for smoother digestion and reducing discomfort. The limonene in the oil has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of difficulty eating. The menthol in the oil has a cooling effect, which can help to numb the pain associated with eating and make it more comfortable.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal essential oils for difficulty eating
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh mint leaves (Mentha x piperita) and 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water over medium heat until it reaches 100°F (38°C).
Add the fresh mint leaves to the water and let it steep for 30 minutes.
Remove the mint leaves and let the mixture cool down. Transfer it to a bowl.
Use a coffee filter or cheesecloth to separate the oil from the water. Collect the oil in a clean container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe digestive issues. The oil also contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant properties that can reduce inflammation and promote healing. Glycyrrhizin helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, making it easier to eat and digest food. The essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the throat and esophagus, making it easier to swallow food. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils can help alleviate difficulty eating caused by digestive issues and discomfort.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal essential oils for difficulty eating
remedy_procedure: Gather materials: 1 cup of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots, a glass jar, a cup, a strainer, and a carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil)
Dry the roots completely in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 2 hours or until they are brittle
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle
Combine the ground roots with 1 cup of carrier oil in the glass jar and stir well. Store the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily
Strain the mixture through the strainer into another cup and discard the solids. Transfer the essential oil to a small glass bottle and store it in a cool, dark place

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils contains a high concentration of camphor, bornyl acetate, and alpha-pinene. These bioactive constituents contribute to its properties that help with difficulty eating. The analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties of camphor and alpha-pinene can reduce pain and discomfort associated with eating, making it easier to consume food. Bornyl acetate has a calming effect, which can help alleviate anxiety and stress related to eating. By reducing pain and anxiety, Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils may be beneficial in facilitating the eating process.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal essential oils for difficulty eating
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves, a clean glass jar with a lid, a cheesecloth or a coffee filter, and a carrier oil like jojoba or sweet almond oil.
Wash the Rosmarinus officinalis leaves with water to remove dirt, then pat them dry with a paper towel to remove excess moisture.
Fill the glass jar with the dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves, and pour 2 cups of carrier oil over them. Stir well to combine.
Place the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the oil extract from the leaves.

After the 2-3 weeks, strain the oil through the cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container. Discard the Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and store the essential oil in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oils contains high levels of limonene, linalool, and beta-pinene as its bioactive constituents. These compounds possess a calming and soothing effect on the digestive system, which can help alleviate difficulties with eating. The anxiolytic properties of linalool and the anti-inflammatory properties of beta-pinene can also contribute to a sense of relaxation, making it easier to eat. The essential oil's ability to reduce anxiety and promote feelings of calmness can also help individuals overcome eating difficulties by reducing stress-related eating issues. By promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing anxiety, Melissa officinalis essential oils can help individuals with difficulty eating to eat more comfortably and easily.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal essential oils for difficulty eating
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Melissa officinalis flowers and 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water over low heat and add the Melissa officinalis flowers. Cover the saucepan.
Let the mixture steep for 2-3 hours or until the water has reduced to about 1 cup.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container. Discard the solids.
Add a few drops of a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil to the liquid and mix well. This is now your Melissa officinalis essential oil.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help ease difficulty eating. Peppermint tea, for example, can calm the stomach and reduce nausea. Ginger tea has anti-inflammatory properties that may soothe digestive issues. These teas may also help with swallowing and digestion by relaxing the muscles involved.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can be a helpful addition to your daily routine when you're having trouble eating. They can help ease digestion and reduce discomfort. Some popular options for this issue include peppermint, ginger, and chamomile teas. These teas are generally safe to drink daily, but it's essential to check the ingredient label for any added sugars or ingredients that might not agree with you.
faq_timeline: The effects of herbal teas on difficulty eating can vary from person to person. Some people may feel relief after a week or two of regular consumption, while others may notice changes after a month. It's not uncommon for people to see improvements in appetite and digestion within a few weeks to a couple of months.
faq_time: For difficulty eating, try drinking herbal tea in the morning or early afternoon. This is when your digestive system is most active. A warm cup can help stimulate your appetite and ease digestion. Choose a calming tea like peppermint or chamomile to help calm your stomach and make mealtime more comfortable.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for difficulty eating. For example, if you're taking medications for depression or anxiety, drinking teas with sedatives like chamomile or valerian root can increase their effects. This may make eating even more difficult. Some teas, like peppermint, may slow digestion and worsen eating issues.
faq_pregnant: Herbal teas might sound like a good idea for eating troubles during pregnancy, but some can be a problem. Certain herbs, like pennyroyal and blue cohosh, can cause issues for mom and baby. Stick to teas that are known to be safe, like peppermint or chamomile. But always check the ingredients and ask the seller about their source.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a helpful aid for difficulty eating while breastfeeding, but it's essential to choose ones that are safe for both you and your baby. Look for teas made from gentle herbs like peppermint, ginger, or chamomile, which can ease digestive issues. Avoid teas that contain caffeine, sage, or pennyroyal, as they can affect milk production or baby's health.
